endpoints.name: POST /providers/Microsoft.Cdn/checkNameAvailability
endpoints.description: Check the availability of a resource name. This is needed for resources where name is globally unique, such as a CDN endpoint.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AZURE_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your AZURE ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: GET /providers/Microsoft.Cdn/edgenodes
endpoints.description: Edgenodes are the global Point of Presence (POP) locations used to deliver CDN content to end users.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AZURE_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your AZURE ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: GET /providers/Microsoft.Cdn/operations
endpoints.description: Lists all of the available CDN REST API operations.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AZURE_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your AZURE ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: POST /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/providers/Microsoft.Cdn/checkNameAvailability
endpoints.description: Check the availability of a resource name. This is needed for resources where name is globally unique, such as a CDN endpoint.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AZURE_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your AZURE ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{SUBSCRIPTIONID}} with a real subscriptionid.

endpoints.name: POST /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/providers/Microsoft.Cdn/checkResourceUsage
endpoints.description: Check the quota and actual usage of the CDN profiles under the given subscription.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AZURE_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your AZURE ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{SUBSCRIPTIONID}} with a real subscriptionid.

endpoints.name: GET /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/providers/Microsoft.Cdn/profiles
endpoints.description: Lists all of the CDN profiles within an Azure subscription.

endpoints.name: POST /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/providers/Microsoft.Cdn/validateProbe
endpoints.description: Check if the probe path is a valid path and the file can be accessed. Probe path is the path to a file hosted on the origin server to help accelerate the delivery of dynamic content via the CDN endpoint. This path is relative to the origin path specified in the endpoint configuration.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AZURE_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your AZURE ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{SUBSCRIPTIONID}} with a real subscriptionid.

endpoints.name: GET /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Cdn/profiles
endpoints.description: Lists all of the CDN profiles within a resource group.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AZURE_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your AZURE ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{SUBSCRIPTIONID}} with a real subscriptionid. Replace {{RESOURCEGROUPNAME}} with a real resourcegroupname.

endpoints.name: GET /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Cdn/profiles/{profileName}
endpoints.description: Gets a CDN profile with the specified profile name under the specified subscription and resource group.

endpoints.name: DELETE /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Cdn/profiles/{profileName}
endpoints.description: Deletes an existing CDN profile with the specified parameters. Deleting a profile will result in the deletion of all of the sub-resources including endpoints, origins and custom domains.

endpoints.description: Updates an existing CDN endpoint with the specified endpoint name under the specified subscription, resource group and profile. endpoints.name: POST /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Cdn/profiles/{profileName}/generateSsoUri
endpoints.description: Generates a dynamic SSO URI used to sign in to the CDN supplemental portal. Supplemental portal is used to configure advanced feature capabilities that are not yet available in the Azure portal, such as core reports in a standard profile; rules engine, advanced HTTP reports, and real-time stats and alerts in a premium profile. The SSO URI changes approximately every 10 minutes.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AZURE_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your AZURE ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{SUBSCRIPTIONID}} with a real subscriptionid. Replace {{PROFILENAME}} with a real profilename. Replace {{RESOURCEGROUPNAME}} with a real resourcegroupname.

endpoints.description: Gets the supported optimization types for the current profile. A user can create an endpoint with an optimization type from the listed values.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AZURE_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your AZURE ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{SUBSCRIPTIONID}} with a real subscriptionid. Replace {{PROFILENAME}} with a real profilename. Replace {{RESOURCEGROUPNAME}} with a real resourcegroupname.
